This acquisition represents a significant expansion for the developer in the battery storage sector, securing a large-scale capacity to support grid stability and energy management within the United Kingdom's energy infrastructure.

This regulatory approval establishes the tariff for the specific solar power procurement intended for use within the state.

This decision applies to solar projects that are commissioned during the 2026-27 financial year. By retaining the current tariff structure, the commission provides regulatory continuity for solar developers planning to complete their projects within the upcoming fiscal period.

tech.headline: Thornova Solar to Integrate Nextpower Steel Frames in US Market
tech.summary: Thornova Solar will integrate Nextpower steel frames into its solar panel manufacturing plans for the U.S. market. Nextpower’s universal steel module frame is designed for broad compatibility with fixed-tilt and tracker mounting systems. Compared to conventional designs, this steel frame improves performance by lowering the risk of glass breakage and reducing module deflection for the panels' bills of materials.

learn.title: Smart Grids and Advanced Metering Systems
learn.intro: Smart grids are revolutionizing energy systems by enabling two-way communication between utilities and consumers, optimizing renewable energy integration, and improving grid reliability. As variable sources like solar and wind grow, smart grids help balance supply and demand dynamically, reducing outages and supporting decarbonization. This digital transformation is critical for achieving net-zero goals.
learn.c1_text: Smart grids use sensors, automation, and data analytics to manage electricity flow in real time, much like the internet manages data traffic. In 2023, over 60% of global electricity demand was served by grids with some smart capabilities, enhancing efficiency and resilience against disruptions.
learn.c2_text: AMI enables two-way communication between utilities and smart meters, providing real-time energy usage data. This allows dynamic pricing, outage detection, and consumer engagement. Over 800 million smart meters were deployed globally by 2023, with China leading at 400 million units.
learn.c3_text: India's Smart Grid Mission aims to modernize its grid infrastructure, targeting 100% smart metering by 2025. The country has installed over 100 million smart meters, focusing on reducing AT&C losses and integrating 450 GW of renewable energy by 2030 into the grid.
learn.stat1: India targets 100% smart metering coverage by 2025.
learn.stat2: Over 800 million smart meters deployed globally as of 2023.
learn.stat3: Smart grids can reduce electricity losses by up to 15% in developing countries.
learn.stat4: IEA projects smart grids will support 70% of global renewable energy integration by 2030.

learn.title: Perovskite Solar Cells: Next-Gen Efficiency
learn.intro: Perovskite solar cells promise higher efficiencies and lower production costs than traditional silicon-based panels. With lab efficiencies surpassing 25%, they offer potential for tandem cells that could exceed 30% efficiency. Their lightweight, flexible nature opens new applications, making them a game-changer for solar energy's future scalability and affordability.
learn.c1_text: Perovskite cells use a crystal structure named after the mineral perovskite, enabling superior light absorption. Lab efficiencies reached 25.7% in 2023, rivaling silicon cells. Tandem structures combining perovskite and silicon could achieve over 30% efficiency, compared to silicon's 22% limit.
learn.c2_text: Perovskite cells can be printed using low-cost methods like inkjet or roll-to-roll processing, reducing manufacturing costs by up to 50%. Their solution-processable nature allows production on flexible substrates, enabling integration into buildings, vehicles, and portable devices.
learn.c3_text: India's IITs and CSIR labs are actively researching perovskite stability and scalability. The country's National Solar Mission includes funding for next-gen solar technologies, aiming to commercialize high-efficiency cells by 2030 to meet its 500 GW renewable target.
learn.stat1: Perovskite-silicon tandem cells achieved 31.3% efficiency in 2023.
learn.stat2: Perovskite manufacturing costs are 30-50% lower than silicon.
learn.stat3: India's solar R&D budget increased by 40% in 2023 for next-gen technologies.
learn.stat4: Global perovskite market projected to reach $1.5 billion by 2030.

learn.title: Green Steel Production via Hydrogen
learn.intro: Green steel production replaces coal with hydrogen in blast furnaces, drastically cutting CO2 emissions. This shift is vital as steel accounts for 7% of global emissions. Hydrogen-based direct reduction processes can reduce emissions by 90%, aligning with climate goals and transforming heavy industry's sustainability landscape.
learn.c1_text: Traditional steelmaking uses coal to reduce iron ore, emitting 2.1 tons of CO2 per ton of steel. Green steel uses hydrogen at 800-1000°C, producing water vapor instead. This process reduces emissions by 90% and requires 50% less energy than coal-based methods.
learn.c2_text: Green steel can cut lifecycle emissions by 95% compared to conventional methods. However, high hydrogen costs (currently $3-6/kg) and infrastructure needs add 20-40% to production costs. Scaling green hydrogen production to $1.5/kg by 2030 is critical for cost parity.
learn.c3_text: India's National Hydrogen Mission targets 5 MMT/year of green hydrogen production by 2030, supporting steel sector decarbonization. Tata Steel and JSW Steel are piloting hydrogen-based DRI plants, aiming to reduce emissions by 30% in their operations by 2030.
learn.stat1: Steel production accounts for 7% of global CO2 emissions.
learn.stat2: Green hydrogen-based steel reduces emissions by 90% vs coal.
learn.stat3: India's steel industry aims for 30% emission reduction by 2030.
learn.stat4: Green hydrogen costs must drop to $1.5/kg for steel cost parity.

learn.title: Floating Solar Farms on Water
learn.intro: Floating solar farms, installed on reservoirs and water bodies, offer dual benefits: clean energy generation and reduced water evaporation. With global potential exceeding 4,000 GW, they address land scarcity while improving panel efficiency through cooling. This innovation supports water conservation and energy security, especially in drought-prone regions.
learn.c1_text: Floating PV systems reduce land use by 70% compared to ground-mounted farms. Water cooling increases panel efficiency by 10-15% due to lower operating temperatures. In India, floating solar projects on reservoirs can prevent 15-20% water evaporation annually, conserving millions of liters.
learn.c2_text: Over 2.6 GW of floating solar was installed globally by 2023, with China leading at 1.5 GW. Countries like India, Thailand, and Brazil are rapidly adopting this technology due to land constraints and water stress. The world's largest floating solar plant, 320 MW, is in China.
learn.c3_text: India has 400 MW of floating solar under development, including a 100 MW project on the Koyna reservoir. The Ministry of New and Renewable Energy promotes floating PV to utilize 1,800 km² of reservoir area, potentially generating 18 GW of clean energy while saving 2,000 million m³ of water annually.
learn.stat1: Global floating solar potential exceeds 4,000 GW.
learn.stat2: Floating PV reduces water evaporation by 15-20% annually.
learn.stat3: India's floating solar projects could save 2,000 million m³ of water yearly.
learn.stat4: World's largest floating solar plant is 320 MW in China.

learn.title: Blue vs Green vs Grey Hydrogen Explained
learn.intro: Hydrogen's 'color code' categorizes production methods by environmental impact. Grey hydrogen uses fossil fuels with high emissions, blue adds carbon capture, and green uses renewable energy. Understanding these distinctions is crucial for policymakers and industries aiming to transition to clean energy while balancing cost and scalability.
learn.c1_text: Grey hydrogen (95% of global supply) emits 10-12 tons of CO2 per ton of H2. Blue hydrogen captures 60-90% of CO2 using CCS, reducing emissions to 2-3 tons. Green hydrogen, made via electrolysis with renewables, emits near-zero CO2 but costs 3-5x more than grey.
learn.c2_text: Grey hydrogen costs $1-2/kg, blue $2-4/kg, and green $4-6/kg today. Scaling green hydrogen to $1.5/kg by 2030 requires massive renewable energy deployment. Blue hydrogen offers a transitional solution but depends on CCS infrastructure, which remains limited globally.
learn.c3_text: India's National Hydrogen Mission prioritizes green hydrogen for steel, fertilizer, and transport sectors. The country plans 5 MMT/year green hydrogen production by 2030, leveraging its 750 GW renewable energy target. Pilot projects in Gujarat and Tamil Nadu aim to reduce green H2 costs to $2/kg by 2025.
learn.stat1: 95% of global hydrogen is grey, emitting 10-12 tons CO2/ton H2.
learn.stat2: Green hydrogen costs 3-5x more than grey today ($4-6/kg vs $1-2/kg).
learn.stat3: India targets 5 MMT/year green hydrogen by 2030.
learn.stat4: Blue hydrogen reduces emissions by 60-90% using CCS technology.

learn.title: Wave Energy Converters: Technologies Overview
learn.intro: Wave energy converters (WECs) harness ocean surface motion to generate electricity, offering predictable, high-energy-density power. With global potential of 2-3 TW, WECs complement wind and solar. Emerging technologies like oscillating water columns and point absorbers are advancing, though commercial viability remains challenging due to harsh marine conditions and costs.
learn.c1_text: Oscillating Water Columns (OWC) use trapped air pressure to drive turbines, while Point Absorbers float and move with waves. Overtopping Devices capture waves in reservoirs, and Attenuators flex along wave crests. OWCs dominate with 40% of global WEC prototypes, offering simplicity and reliability.
learn.c2_text: WECs could generate 2-3 TW globally, enough to power 15 billion homes. However, technical challenges like corrosion resistance, mooring systems, and maintenance in harsh seas increase costs. Current LCOE ranges from $0.20-0.40/kWh, 3-5x higher than offshore wind.
learn.c3_text: India's Ocean Energy Research Centre focuses on WEC prototypes along its 7,500 km coastline. Pilot projects in Tamil Nadu and Gujarat aim to harness 10 GW of wave energy potential. The government allocated $10 million in 2023 for testing OWC and attenuator designs, targeting 1 GW deployment by 2030.
learn.stat1: Global wave energy potential is 2-3 TW, enough for 15 billion homes.
learn.stat2: WEC LCOE is $0.20-0.40/kWh, 3-5x higher than offshore wind.
learn.stat3: India's coastline has 10 GW of wave energy potential.
learn.stat4: Over 40% of WEC prototypes worldwide are Oscillating Water Columns.
